req.title: Interest accrual (negative variant)
req.statement: A savings account shall accrue interest daily using the tier rate and credit interest monthly on the last business day. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-066 mandates daily tier-based interest accrual with monthly credit on the last business day via /cron/interest. Five tests cover: (1) EP positive – eligible account receives correct interest credit and ledger entry; (2) NEG – account with zero/insufficient balance triggers reconciliation variance; (3) BVA – balance exactly at tier boundary accrues at the lower tier rate; (4) STC – job idempotency: re-running on the same day must not double-credit; (5) DT – unauthorised/locked account is skipped and variance is reported. Each test is self-contained using fixtures and helpers, deterministic via controlled seed data, and idempotent via teardown or unique account IDs.

tests.name: test_interest_credited_eligible_account
preconditions: Savings account exists with balance in the standard tier (e.g. 10000.00)
Today is the last business day of the month
No prior interest credit exists for this month
actions: POST /cron/interest with valid cron auth header and current month-end date
Retrieve account ledger entries via GET /accounts/{account_id}/ledger
expected: POST /cron/interest returns HTTP 200
Response body contains account_id with status 'credited'
Ledger contains exactly one INTEREST_CREDIT entry for the month
Credited amount equals balance * daily_tier_rate * days_in_month (within 0.01 tolerance)
Account balance increased by the credited interest amount
trace: R-FIN-066: interest credited monthly on last business day
tests.executable:
import pytest
import math
from decimal import Decimal

def test_interest_credited_eligible_account(banking_api_client, seed_savings_account, month_end_date):
    account_id, initial_balance, tier_rate, days = seed_savings_account(balance=10000.00)
    expected_interest = round(initial_balance * tier_rate * days, 2)

    resp = banking_api_client.post(
        "/cron/interest",
        json={"run_date": month_end_date},
        headers=<cron_auth_header>
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    results = {r["account_id"]: r for r in resp.json()["results"]}
    assert account_id in results
    assert results[account_id]["status"] == "credited"

    ledger = banking_api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/ledger").json()
    interest_entries = [e for e in ledger if e["type"] == "INTEREST_CREDIT" and e["period"] == month_end_date[:7]]
    assert len(interest_entries) == 1
    assert abs(Decimal(str(interest_entries[0]["amount"])) - Decimal(str(expected_interest))) <= Decimal("0.01")

    balance_resp = banking_api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}").json()
    assert abs(Decimal(str(balance_resp["balance"])) - Decimal(str(initial_balance + expected_interest))) <= Decimal("0.01")

tests.name: test_interest_zero_balance_reports_reconciliation_variance
preconditions: Savings account exists with balance of 0.00
Today is the last business day of the month
actions: POST /cron/interest with valid cron auth header and current month-end date
expected: POST /cron/interest returns HTTP 200 (job completes without crash)
Account result status is 'skipped' or 'variance'
No INTEREST_CREDIT ledger entry is created for this account
Reconciliation report lists the account under 'variances' with reason 'INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE'
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_interest_zero_balance_reports_reconciliation_variance(banking_api_client, seed_savings_account, month_end_date):
    account_id, _, _, _ = seed_savings_account(balance=0.00)

    resp = banking_api_client.post(
        "/cron/interest",
        json={"run_date": month_end_date},
        headers=<cron_auth_header>
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    results = {r["account_id"]: r for r in resp.json()["results"]}
    assert account_id in results
    assert results[account_id]["status"] in ("skipped", "variance")

    ledger = banking_api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/ledger").json()
    interest_entries = [e for e in ledger if e["type"] == "INTEREST_CREDIT" and e["period"] == month_end_date[:7]]
    assert len(interest_entries) == 0

    recon = banking_api_client.get(f"/cron/interest/reconciliation/{month_end_date}").json()
    variance_ids = [v["account_id"] for v in recon["variances"]]
    assert account_id in variance_ids
    matched = next(v for v in recon["variances"] if v["account_id"] == account_id)
    assert matched["reason"] == "INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE"

tests.name: test_interest_balance_exactly_at_tier_boundary
preconditions: System has two tiers: Tier1 rate for balance < 10000.00, Tier2 rate for balance >= 10000.00
Savings account exists with balance exactly 10000.00 (lower boundary of Tier2)
Today is the last business day of the month
actions: POST /cron/interest with valid cron auth header and current month-end date
Compute expected interest using Tier2 rate
expected: POST /cron/interest returns HTTP 200
Account status is 'credited'
Exactly one INTEREST_CREDIT ledger entry exists for the month
Credited amount matches balance * TIER2_daily_rate * days_in_month (within 0.01)
Tier1 rate is NOT used for the calculation
trace: R-FIN-066: tier rate applied – boundary between Tier1 and Tier2
tests.executable:
import pytest
from decimal import Decimal

TIER2_BOUNDARY = 10000.00

def test_interest_balance_exactly_at_tier_boundary(banking_api_client, seed_savings_account, month_end_date, tier_rates):
    tier1_rate, tier2_rate = tier_rates["tier1"], tier_rates["tier2"]
    days = <days_in_month>(month_end_date)
    account_id, _, _, _ = seed_savings_account(balance=TIER2_BOUNDARY)

    resp = banking_api_client.post(
        "/cron/interest",
        json={"run_date": month_end_date},
        headers=<cron_auth_header>
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    results = {r["account_id"]: r for r in resp.json()["results"]}
    assert results[account_id]["status"] == "credited"

    expected_t2 = round(TIER2_BOUNDARY * tier2_rate * days, 2)
    unexpected_t1 = round(TIER2_BOUNDARY * tier1_rate * days, 2)

    ledger = banking_api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/ledger").json()
    entries = [e for e in ledger if e["type"] == "INTEREST_CREDIT" and e["period"] == month_end_date[:7]]
    assert len(entries) == 1
    credited = Decimal(str(entries[0]["amount"]))
    assert abs(credited - Decimal(str(expected_t2))) <= Decimal("0.01")
    assert abs(credited - Decimal(str(unexpected_t1))) > Decimal("0.01")

tests.name: test_interest_job_idempotent_no_double_credit
preconditions: Savings account exists with eligible balance (5000.00)
Today is the last business day of the month
System job has already been run once for this month-end date (state: COMPLETED)
actions: POST /cron/interest for the same run_date a second time
expected: Second POST returns HTTP 200 or HTTP 409 (idempotent or conflict, not 500)
Response body indicates job already processed ('already_run' or 'no_op')
Ledger still contains exactly ONE INTEREST_CREDIT entry for the month (no duplication)
Account balance has not increased beyond the first run's credited amount
trace: R-FIN-066: monthly credit on last business day – must not credit twice
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_interest_job_idempotent_no_double_credit(banking_api_client, seed_savings_account, month_end_date):
    account_id, initial_balance, _, _ = seed_savings_account(balance=5000.00)

    first = banking_api_client.post(
        "/cron/interest",
        json={"run_date": month_end_date},
        headers=<cron_auth_header>
    )
    assert first.status_code == 200

    balance_after_first = banking_api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}").json()["balance"]

    second = banking_api_client.post(
        "/cron/interest",
        json={"run_date": month_end_date},
        headers=<cron_auth_header>
    )
    assert second.status_code in (200, 409)
    if second.status_code == 200:
        assert second.json().get("status") in ("already_run", "no_op")

    ledger = banking_api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/ledger").json()
    interest_entries = [e for e in ledger if e["type"] == "INTEREST_CREDIT" and e["period"] == month_end_date[:7]]
    assert len(interest_entries) == 1

    balance_after_second = banking_api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}").json()["balance"]
    assert balance_after_second == balance_after_first

tests.name: test_interest_locked_account_skipped_variance_reported
preconditions: Savings account exists with eligible balance (8000.00) but account status is LOCKED/UNAUTHORISED
Today is the last business day of the month
actions: POST /cron/interest with valid cron auth header and current month-end date
Retrieve ledger entries for locked account
expected: POST /cron/interest returns HTTP 200 (job does not crash on locked account)
Locked account appears in reconciliation report variances with reason 'ACCOUNT_LOCKED' or 'UNAUTHORISED'
No INTEREST_CREDIT ledger entry created for the locked account
Other eligible accounts in same run are not affected
trace: R-FIN-066: exceeded limits / locked state decision
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_interest_locked_account_skipped_variance_reported(banking_api_client, seed_savings_account, seed_locked_account, month_end_date):
    eligible_id, _, _, _ = seed_savings_account(balance=5000.00)
    locked_id = seed_locked_account(balance=8000.00)

    resp = banking_api_client.post(
        "/cron/interest",
        json={"run_date": month_end_date},
        headers=<cron_auth_header>
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    results = {r["account_id"]: r for r in resp.json()["results"]}
    assert results.get(locked_id, {}).get("status") in ("skipped", "variance", None)

    recon = banking_api_client.get(f"/cron/interest/reconciliation/{month_end_date}").json()
    variance_ids = [v["account_id"] for v in recon["variances"]]
    assert locked_id in variance_ids
    matched = next(v for v in recon["variances"] if v["account_id"] == locked_id)
    assert matched["reason"] in ("ACCOUNT_LOCKED", "UNAUTHORISED")

    ledger = banking_api_client.get(f"/accounts/{locked_id}/ledger").json()
    interest_entries = [e for e in ledger if e["type"] == "INTEREST_CREDIT" and e["period"] == month_end_date[:7]]
    assert len(interest_entries) == 0

    assert results.get(eligible_id, {}).get("status") == "credited"
